name: Brisket with Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch slices
1 1/2 pounds parsnips, peeled and cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a pressure cooker.
2. Brown the beef brisket well on both sides over high heat, lifting it occasionally with tongs or a spatula to prevent sticking. Add more fat if needed. Set aside.
3. In the remaining oil, sauté celery, carrots, and onion for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping up any browned bits.
4. Add beef stock, water, cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice to the pot. Place a rack into the liquid and put the meat on top with the fat side facing up.
5. Seal the lid and bring the cooker to high pressure over high heat. Maintain high pressure for 55 minutes.
6. Allow the pressure to drop naturally for about 10-15 minutes. Remove the lid, tilting it away from you to let out any excess steam.
7. If the brisket is tender enough, use a fork to pry off a small piece of meat. If not, return the meat and liquid to high pressure for another 10-15 minutes.
8. Cool the brisket and gravy to room temperature, then refrigerate if time allows.
9. About 20 minutes before serving, stir in cider until it reaches about 2 cups total volume. Add sweet potatoes, parsnips, and prunes. Seal the lid and bring to high pressure over high heat. Cook for 3 minutes.
10. Quick-release the pressure and season with salt and pepper.
11. Slice the brisket against the grain and place in the cooker with gravy and vegetables. Cover but do not lock the lid. Cook over medium heat until the meat is warmed throughout.
12. Serve on a platter, surrounding with vegetables, and pour gravy over top.
